clamp_strategy: Use a vendor-neutral clamp envelope until authorized tooling geometry exists.
failure_modes: Purchased-tool geometry incorrectly enters authored manufacturing output.
Clamp motion blocks loading or weld access.
reaction_strategy: Map the clamp contact to a named support or locator reaction.
selection_criteria: Open and closed envelopes are clear.
Clamp force and vendor selection remain human review.
records.summary: Represent purchased clamps by provisional motion and contact envelopes, never invented release geometry.
records.title: Vendor-neutral clamp review envelope

failure_modes: Support is not under the clamped region.
records.summary: Use discrete, serviceable supports at product-specific stable regions.
support_strategy: Three primary rests establish the support plane without contour-skeleton geometry.
records.title: Replaceable product support

failure_modes: Broad locator face overconstrains a variable product surface.
locator_strategy: Use a narrow, relieved, replaceable contact tied to the accepted secondary datum.
selection_criteria: Adjustment and prove-out evidence are explicit.
records.summary: Apply a discrete secondary locator rather than a generic wall or contour copy.
records.title: Relieved secondary locator

failure_modes: Stop is mistaken for a clamp.
selection_criteria: Stop references an immutable product feature.
stop_strategy: Use a replaceable end or radial stop for the remaining in-plane translation.
records.summary: Separate tertiary stopping from support and clamping roles.
records.title: Product-bound tertiary stop

base_strategy: Tie local nests to a continuous base with explicit table mounting.
failure_modes: Fixture islands have no shared load path.
selection_criteria: Fastener and table standard remain engineer-confirmed.
records.summary: Make the table interface visible and traceable without claiming a universal hole standard.
records.title: Explicit table mounting

base_strategy: Use proportionate end gussets or triangulated braces outside product and access corridors.
failure_modes: Decorative brace has no connected parent path.
selection_criteria: Brace is connected to base and rail.
records.summary: Use compact triangulation where the continuous base needs an explicit end load path.
records.title: Compact end triangulation

base_strategy: Compact continuous table-mounted base with connected local nests and proportionate end structure.
clamp_strategy: Clamps act near named supports and open away from the load corridor.
constrained_dofs: secondary locator: lateral translation and yaw
failure_modes: Any product-specific contact, reaction mapping, load sequence, or connected-base evidence becomes absent.
floating_dofs: intended release direction after clamps open
load_unload: Open clamps, load to primary supports, engage secondary locator, seat tertiary stop, clamp, tack or weld as approved, release, unload.
reaction_strategy: Every clamp names a reaction support.
records.summary: Abstract target direction: compact, coherent, product-bound, explainable, loadable, weld-accessible, and shop-buildable.
records.title: Accepted M32 repair direction

base_strategy: long narrow rail with weakly integrated station islands
clamp_strategy: generic repeated clamps without product contact or reaction evidence
load_unload: unclear or purely textual loading sequence
records.summary: Reject generic station blocks distributed along excessive empty rail without coherent fixture-engineering intent.
records.title: Rejected generic M32 arrangement

base_strategy: One continuous low base or paired rail frame connects all stations and table mounts.
selection_criteria: All stations have a parent path to one mounted base.
Length follows occupied stations and service clearances.
station_repetition: Repeat one validated nest while retaining stable source references per station.
records.summary: A compact continuous mounted structure unifies repeated product nests.
records.title: Compact continuous multi-station base

constrained_dofs: three primary contacts support a plane
secondary and tertiary contacts remove intended remaining motion
failure_modes: Generic blocks have no feature reason.
records.summary: Build a product-specific nest from a small number of explicit, replaceable contacts.
support_strategy: Use discrete supports, not a complete product contour.
records.title: Discrete product-bound station nest

clamp_strategy: Apply clamp force toward a supported product region.
failure_modes: Clamp and support identities cannot be paired.
reaction_strategy: Name one support or locator reaction for each clamp.
records.summary: Place and orient each clamp so its load resolves into fixture-supported product geometry.
records.title: Clamp-to-support reaction pair

load_unload: Define load, locate, clamp, process, release, and unload as separate checked states.
records.summary: Arrange nests and clamp motion around a coherent, explicitly checked handling sequence.
records.title: Coherent load-clamp-unload flow

cleaning_maintenance: Keep spatter-exposed contacts replaceable and reachable.
distortion_heat_spatter: Separate tack access from full-weld approval and record heat/spatter assumptions.
failure_modes: Clamp or structure blocks a required joint.
Full-weld access is claimed from unconfirmed joint evidence.
records.summary: Reserve a visible process corridor around confirmed joints and keep access claims fail-closed.
records.title: Protected weld and maintenance corridor
weld_access: Check clamp, base, adjacent station, and current-product candidates separately.

changeover: Replace wear contacts without redefining the datum hierarchy.
failure_modes: Service requires cutting apart the fixture.
station_repetition: Repeat stable roles and identities; preserve station-specific source references.
records.summary: Repeated stations retain one role model while replaceable contacts support prove-out and maintenance.
records.title: Serviceable repeated station pattern

constrained_dofs: Use the minimum independent contacts needed for the intended six-degree-of-freedom restraint.
failure_modes: Redundant locating contacts fight product variation.
floating_dofs: Directions needed for loading, thermal response, or release remain explicit.
records.summary: Define an explicit primary, secondary, and tertiary datum strategy and trace controlled motion.
records.title: Datum hierarchy and constrained motion

clamp_strategy: Clamping holds a located part; it does not create the datum scheme.
records.summary: Seat and locate the product before applying hold-down force.
records.title: Locate before clamp

failure_modes: Clamp bends unsupported sheet or fabricated members.
reaction_strategy: Direct each clamp force into a named support or structurally supported region.
records.summary: Make clamp reaction paths explicit and reject unsupported loading.
records.title: Support every clamp reaction

failure_modes: Orientation error is detectable only after clamping.
foolproof_strategy: Use an asymmetric, visible, non-damaging load cue where reversal is plausible.
records.summary: Consider and visibly address wrong-way or incomplete loading.
records.title: Foolproof product loading

load_unload: Prove complete entry and exit paths.
records.summary: Protect operator, tool, weld, maintenance, and completed-product release access.
records.title: Access and release remain first-class

failure_modes: Generic contact has no relationship to accepted product evidence.
locator_strategy: Tie contacts to stable immutable product references and explain each role.
records.summary: Every support, locator, stop, and clamp contact needs a product-specific application reason.
records.title: Product-specific contact evidence

base_strategy: Use connected structure and explicit table mounting; do not infer certified capacity.
failure_modes: Station islands lack a common load path.
records.summary: Connect fixture elements into a coherent mounted base strategy while leaving strength approval to qualified review.
records.title: Coherent mounted structure

cleaning_maintenance: Expose wear items and spatter zones for service.
failure_modes: Wear contact is permanent and inaccessible.
Adjustment cannot be locked or revalidated.
records.summary: Make wear, adjustment, cleaning, and changeover intent explicit.
records.title: Maintainable and replaceable tooling
